title: Generate Parentheses
prompt:
Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses. Return the combinations sorted lexicographically.

A well-formed parenthesis string has every opening '(' matched with a closing ')' and they are properly nested.

Example: n = 3 → ["((()))","(()())","(())()","()(())","()()()"].

notesForAI.coreIdea: Backtracking with two counters: open_count and close_count. Add '(' if open_count < n. Add ')' if close_count < open_count. Base case: string length == 2*n.
commonBugs: Allowing close_count to exceed open_count (produces invalid strings)
Generating all 2^(2n) strings and filtering (too slow)
Using string concatenation in a loop instead of a mutable list/builder
notesForAI.expectedComplexity: O(4^n / sqrt(n)) — the nth Catalan number
